Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay The Present Litigation
Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Dr Maher Memarzadeh, (310) 310-5584, maher@ucla.edu, Plaintiff in Propria Persona
Val D. Hornstein, Esq., HORNSTEIN LAW, A Prof. Corp. (415) 454-1490, for Defendant in Pro per,

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT HORNSTEIN LAW OFFICES, PROF. CORP.'S Motion To Stay The Present Litigation.

Defendants' "motion to stay the present litigation" pending final resolution of writ action CPF-22-517700 is granted. Whether or not wholly dispositive of the present litigation, the outcome of the writ action should be relevant. A regular noticed motion shall be required to lift the stay. "A court ordinarily has inherent power, in its discretion, to stay proceedings when a stay will accommodate the ends of justice." (OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
